text: Let me read the test output and get more precise data.
thinking: Now let me parse the test output to understand which tests passed/failed and which modules have tests. Let me read the saved test output.

tool_uses.name: Bash
tool_uses.input:
{"command": "cd /Users/gladyscavero/Documents/PEOPL/GitHub/claw-code/rust && for crate_dir in crates/*/; do
  crate=$(basename "$crate_dir")
  src_files=$(find "$crate_dir/src" -name "*.rs" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  test_files=$(find "$crate_dir/tests" -name "*.rs" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  # Count #[test] and #[tokio::test] in src files (inline tests)
  inline_tests=$(grep -r '#\[test\]\|#\[tokio::test\]' "$crate_dir/src" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  # Count #[test] in test files
  ext_tests=$(grep -r '#\[test\]\|#\[tokio::test\]' "$crate_dir/tests" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  # Count src lines
  src_lines=$(find "$crate_dir/src" -name "*.rs" -exec cat {} + 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  echo "$crate|$src_files|$test_files|$inline_tests|$ext_tests|$src_lines"
done", "description": "Count source files, test files, and test counts per crate"}

tool_uses.name: Bash
tool_uses.input:
{"command": "cd /Users/gladyscavero/Documents/PEOPL/GitHub/claw-code/rust && for f in crates/*/src/*.rs; do
  mod=$(echo "$f" | sed 's|crates/||;s|/src/|::|;s|\.rs$||')
  has_test_mod=$(grep -c '#\[cfg(test)\]' "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  test_count=$(grep -c '#\[test\]\|#\[tokio::test\]' "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  lines=$(wc -l < "$f" | tr -d ' ')
  echo "$mod|$lines|$has_test_mod|$test_count"
done", "description": "Get per-module test coverage info"}
